description: Tests agentic prompt quality — stop conditions, scope, safety, state management

tests.name: Simple agent task
tests.prompt: Write a Claude Code prompt to add unit tests to the auth module
assertions: Includes starting state (existing test framework, auth module location)
Includes target state (which functions get tests, coverage target)
Includes stop conditions
Includes file scope (only auth module, not other code)
Includes progress output instructions

tests.name: Multi-step agent task
tests.prompt: Write a Claude Code prompt to migrate a project from JavaScript to TypeScript
assertions: Breaks into sequential steps
Each step has its own scope and stop conditions
Includes rollback strategy
Includes human review triggers
Does NOT try to do everything in one prompt

tests.name: Destructive agent task
tests.prompt: Write a Devin prompt to delete all unused dependencies from a large Node.js project
assertions: Includes explicit forbidden actions (no deleting src code, no modifying configs)
Requires confirmation before each deletion
Includes backup/restore instructions
Scope locked to package.json and node_modules only

tests.name: Agent with external API
tests.prompt: Write a Claude Code prompt to set up Stripe payment integration in a Next.js app
assertions: Includes starting state (Next.js project structure)
Includes target state (Stripe checkout flow working)
Notes that Stripe API key is needed
Does NOT hardcode API keys in the prompt
Includes human review before going live

tests.name: Agent scope control
tests.prompt: Write a Cursor prompt to optimize the database queries in my Express.js API. There are 50 routes — only optimize the /api/users routes.
assertions: Locks scope to /api/users routes only
Lists forbidden routes/files
Includes 'Done When' condition
Does NOT try to optimize all 50 routes

tests.name: Agent error handling
tests.prompt: Write a Devin prompt to set up CI/CD with GitHub Actions for a Python project
assertions: Includes error recovery steps
Includes what to do if tests fail
Includes what to do if deployment fails
Stop conditions for each failure mode

tests.name: Agent with state management
tests.prompt: Write a Claude Code prompt to refactor a monolith into microservices over multiple sessions
assertions: Includes session strategy (new session per service)
Includes checkpoint/state saving between sessions
Each session has clear scope and stop conditions
Includes progress tracking across sessions

tests.name: Agent safety (no deploy)
tests.prompt: Write a Cline prompt to update the production database schema
assertions: Requires human approval before any database changes
Includes rollback plan
Specifies exact changes (not 'improve the schema')
Does NOT auto-deploy

tests.name: Agent with testing requirement
tests.prompt: Write a Claude Code prompt to add a new API endpoint with full test coverage
assertions: Includes implementation + test writing in scope
Specifies test framework and coverage target
Stop condition: all tests pass
Does NOT skip testing

tests.name: Agent session hygiene
tests.prompt: Write a Claude Code prompt for a complex refactoring task. I've been working on this for 50 turns and context is getting huge.
assertions: Recommends starting a new session
Suggests /compact or /rewind
Provides context summary to carry forward
Does NOT try to continue in the bloated session
